review_notes: Manual GPT Acquired batch v5.1 — Season 7/8 target batch | optimized v5.4
conclusion: DoorDash's 2020 IPO caps seven years from paloaltodelivery.com (2013) — Stanford Design Garage project with $6 flat fees and Find My Friends tracking — to 50% US food delivery share (Oct 2020) from 17% (Jan 2018). Suburban San Jose beat dense cities; one in six Peninsula residents ordered by early 2014. SoftBank $535M at $1.4B (38% dilution) followed $60M Sequoia bridge (2017). 2019: $885M net revenue (3X YoY), $8B GOV, 263M orders, 60% same-store sales growth. Pandemic March 2020: 20%+ GBV growth in one month off $8B base. DashPass: 5M members at $10/month. Capital-intensive delivery may work if gig economics and Prop 22 hold — Alfred Lin's second Sequoia IPO in two days after Airbnb. Keith Rabois Khosla $2.4M seed when Sand Hill scoffed at food delivery category. Uber raised ~$8B lifetime; DoorDash won US share with hundreds of millions plus operational excellence at lowest level of detail on suburban routing and restaurant onboarding queues and parking logistics.
background: Tony Xu, Stanley Tang, Andy Fang, and Evan Moore start Palo Alto Delivery in Stanford's Design Garage class (2012–13), inspired by Square's smartphone-enabled merchants and absent suburban delivery outside pizza chains. Grubhub/Seamless owned NYC with restaurant-employed couriers — capital-light but geographically limited. DoorDash owned fulfillment with 1099 drivers and $6 flat fees. YC summer 2013 → $2.4M seed (Keith Rabois/Khosla). Series B: John Doerr, $40M at $600M (2015). SoftBank $535M at $1.4B (38% dilution), 2017 $60M Sequoia bridge, recovery to $600M at $12.6B (2019). Caviar acquired from Square. S-1 reveals brutal dilution while share rose 17%→50% 2018–2020. Recorded alongside Airbnb IPO week — Alfred Lin's second Sequoia portfolio IPO in two days. Grubhub passed orders to restaurant couriers; DoorDash owned suburban 1099 fulfillment — category insight that unlocked national Yum! chain partnerships after Peninsula land-grab success.
important_facts: DoorDash US market share grew from 17% (January 2018) to 50% (October 2020) — surpassing Uber Eats, Grubhub, and Postmates in roughly 2¾ years per S-1 analysis cited in episode.
2019 metrics: $885 million net revenue (more than 3X year-on-year), $8 billion gross order value, 263 million orders, and 60% same-store sales growth excluding new markets and restaurant additions.

SoftBank invested $535 million in 2018 at $1.4 billion post-money valuation — 38% dilution with per-share price still below John Doerr's 2015 Series B at $600 million post despite higher headline valuation.

By early 2014 one in six San Francisco Bay Peninsula residents had used DoorDash — roughly one year after first paloaltodelivery.com delivery; Tony Xu, Stanley Tang, Andy Fang started in Stanford Design Garage class (2012–13) with $6 flat delivery fees.

DashPass membership reached 5 million subscribers at $10 per month — ~$600 million annual run-rate if fully paid. Caviar acquired from Square for ~$410M expanded premium segment. Sequoia provided $60M insider bridge end-2017 when cash exhausted; Alfred Lin committed up to $40M pro-rata at $1B cap before SoftBank round.

mental_model.name: Suburban Fulfillment at Lowest-Level Detail
mental_model.components: Food delivery is three-sided (consumer, restaurant, driver) versus rideshare's two-sided match — parking, in-store queues, and apartment drops dominate unit economics. DoorDash won suburbs where Grubhub's restaurant-courier model failed. Gig 1099 labor variabilizes off-peak cost. DashPass bundles habit (5M members, $10/month, ~$600M run-rate); Yum! nationalizes after Peninsula grind where one in six residents ordered by early 2014. Average delivery time distributions matter — 53-minute tail kills NPS even if mean is 35. SoftBank $535M at $1.4B (38% dilution) funded share war; Sequoia $60M bridge end-2017 saved company. Meituan China proof point reversed US skepticism.
mental_model.application: Map real-world ops before TAM models — suburbs beat cities for delivery throughput. Watch dilution: 2018 SoftBank 38% sale versus 2019 <5% raise signals leverage cycles. Compare Meituan when US skeptics peak. Tony's Square DNA and Keith Rabois $2.4M Khosla seed explain funding when Sand Hill scoffed. DashPass 5M members at $10/month (~$600M run-rate) and Yum! partnerships nationalize after Peninsula land-grab where one in six residents ordered within a year. Uber raised ~$8B lifetime; DoorDash won with hundreds of millions plus suburban operational excellence at lowest level of detail.

competitive_advantage: DoorDash's edge is suburban operational obsession — parking, routing (early Find My Friends courier tracking), and restaurant onboarding — plus owning 1099 courier fulfillment while Grubhub passed orders to restaurant drivers. Share rose 17% (Jan 2018) to 50% (Oct 2020) in ~2¾ years. Sequoia bridges ($60M end-2017 when cash exhausted; Alfred Lin up to $40M pro-rata at $1B cap) plus SoftBank $535M at $1.4B post (38% dilution) funded land-grab. DashPass: 5M members at $10/month (~$600M run-rate). 2019: $885M net revenue (3X YoY), $8B GOV, 263M orders, 60% same-store sales growth. Pandemic March 2020: 20%+ GBV growth in one month off $8B base. Yum! Brands partnerships nationalize after Peninsula land-grab where one in six residents ordered by early 2014. Weaknesses: Prop 22 gig-worker risk; extraordinary dilution (Khosla/CRV seed fell below 5% S-1); thin margins. Capital table: $2.4M seed, $40M Series B at $600M (Doerr), $535M SoftBank 2018, $600M at $12.6B (2019). Uber raised ~$8B lifetime; DoorDash won with hundreds of millions plus ops excellence. Tony Xu's Square internship and Keith Rabois Khosla seed ($2.4M) explain capital access when Sand Hill scoffed at food delivery. Prop 22 (California) preserved 1099 model underpinning variable labor economics. Khosla/CRV seed investors fell below 5% ownership in S-1 — extraordinary dilution path from $2.4M seed to $12.6B valuation. Capital intensity means share gains from 17% to 50% in ~2¾ years must justify billions raised versus Uber Eats and Grubhub.

key_insights.view: Suburbs beat cities for delivery ops.
key_insights.question: Why launch San Jose not SF?
key_insights.answer: Dense cities mean parking tickets, restaurant queues, high-rise drops — killing throughput versus San Jose suburban routes. Tony: consumers remember 53-minute delivery tails not 35-minute means. Peninsula land-grab hit one-in-six residents by early 2014 within ~one year of first paloaltodelivery.com delivery. Early Find My Friends iPhone tracking was hacky real-time ops before dedicated logistics stack — Square internship DNA informed merchant onboarding.

key_insights.view: Fulfillment ownership was the category insight.
key_insights.question: How was Grubhub different?
key_insights.answer: Grubhub/Seamless (profitable, public since early 2000s) passed orders to restaurant-employed couriers — worked in NYC bike density, not Palo Alto sprawl. DoorDash 1099 drivers unlocked suburbs and national chains like Yum! (Taco Bell, KFC). Started with $6 flat delivery fee in Stanford Design Garage class (2012–13); DashPass $10/month now reduces checkout friction for 5M members (~$600M run-rate if fully paid).

key_insights.view: SoftBank round was dilution shock then catalyst.
key_insights.question: What happened in 2018?
key_insights.answer: $535M at $1.4B post sold 38% — per-share price below 2015 Kleiner Series B at $600M post despite higher headline valuation. Funded share war vs Uber Eats and Postmates; Meituan China proof gave SoftBank conviction after US food-delivery skepticism. March 2019 raised $250M at less than 5% dilution — leverage returned after competitors stumbled. Caviar $410M from Square expanded premium segment alongside core suburban routes.

key_insights.question: Who saved the company in 2017?
key_insights.answer: S-1 revealed $60M insider bridge when cash exhausted end of 2017 — Sequoia kept DoorDash alive pre-SoftBank round. Alfred Lin committed up to $40M pro-rata at $1B cap before external pricer. Built ~18% IPO stake through bridges when Sand Hill skeptics passed on food delivery — Alfred Lin's second Sequoia portfolio IPO in two days alongside Airbnb that same December 2020 week.

key_insights.view: Pandemic was hyper-growth on huge base.
key_insights.question: How big was COVID bump?
key_insights.answer: March 2020 delivered 20%+ GBV growth in one month off $8B+ prior-year GOV — seed-stage velocity at scale rarely seen. Share rose 17%→50% by October 2020 while Uber Eats grew slower. 2019 baseline: $885M net revenue (3X YoY), 263M orders, 60% same-store sales growth. DashPass 5M members at $10/month; Prop 22 preserved California 1099 model underpinning variable labor economics.

top_investment_implications.thesis: 50% US share and DashPass habit offset capital intensity; monitor gig regulation and post-pandemic order retention vs. 2019's 3X revenue growth baseline.
Uber Eats lost share as DoorDash hit 50% — read on whether rides business can subsidize delivery or if separation is inevitable.
Grubhub's capital-light restaurant-courier model lost to fulfillment-first DoorDash — legacy profitable aggregator vs. growth-share loser.

golden_quotes: Averages in our industry are meaningless — Tony Xu on delivery-time distributions (quoted by David).
One in six people on the Peninsula had used DoorDash — David on 2014 suburban land-grab metrics.
We are a logistics company more so than a food company — DoorDash 2013 Medium post after $2.4M seed.

YC batch; renamed DoorDash; $2.4M seed led by Khosla
1 in 6 Peninsula residents orders; San Jose expansion
$40M Series B from Kleiner at $600M; John Doerr joins board
$885M revenue; passes Grubhub; buys Caviar from Square
IPO; market share reaches 50% by October
